programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Victoria Fritz present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage of the world's biggest half-marathon, which sees tens of thousands of runners of all abilities negotiate the course from Newcastle upon Tyne to South Shields. British favourite Mo Farah won the men's elite race for a record fifth time in 2018, beating New Zealand's Jake Robertson into second place as he did 12 months earlier. A home favourite also prevailed in the men's wheelchair race, with David Weir claiming his seventh title here, while Kenyan Vivian Cheruiyot won the elite women's event for the second time in three years and Poland's Martyna Snopek was the women's wheelchair champion(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores sacred architecture 350 years after Christopher Wren started work on St Paul's Cathedral. Josie d'Arby meets Christian artists collective Morphe Arts(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an active couple return to their roots in Shropshire, looking for a property within their £350,000 budget that gives them the great outdoors on their doorstep(n)

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens (2009)
programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore reclaims three items from a Walsall recycling centre, with designer Rupert Blanchard given the task of bringing a boring bookcase into the 21st century(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Kelly Cates, Adrian Chiles, Andy Hamilton, Kate Williams, Mark Dolan, Rustie Lee, Esther Rantzen and Tim Vine(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Northumberland, where Helen Skelton visits a scheme in Blyth in which students from disadvantaged areas work with retired engineers and craftsmen to learn traditional boat-building and shipwright skills. Adam Henson meets the next contestant hoping to lift the trophy in this year's One Man and His Dog competition and Matt Baker has all the information on how viewers can take part in this year's Countryfile Ramble for Children in Need(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Dover Castle for a special edition marking 80 years since the start of the Second World War, bringing insights into the conflict through personal stories and family mementos. A photo album belonging to an Austrian soldier offers a previously unseen glimpse of Neville Chamberlain's ill-fated meeting with Adolf Hitler in 1938, while Fiona Bruce meets 95-year-old Ray Palmer, who has the rare distinction of being both a child evacuee and then a serving soldier(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy waits by the water at Charlie's Yard to meet a dangerous adversary, but despite their past grievances the pair agree to a truce. Tommy receives a message from a mysterious stranger during his constituency surgery, while at his Westminster office, the MP makes Aberama a very tempting offer, and later invites Mosley to a party he is throwing for Lizzie's birthday. On the night of the event, things soon take a turn for the worse when a surprise guest arrives. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding returns with more appeals for information about crimes, with roving reporter Michelle Ackerley going behind the scenes with police services around the UK, beginning with the West Yorkshire force(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits houses in Stourbridge and Liverpool, and there is a look at a Grade II- listed property in Brentwood, Essex, that Martin Roberts first saw back in 2016(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers check hygiene standards in Oxford takeaways while in Studley, Warwickshire, they crack down on a butcher claiming meat was British when it came from overseas(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A glider pilot's footage captures the moments after he got stuck in a tree, as well as his rescue, and a DJ dad relives the night a car failed to stop at a notorious crossroads(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, as Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help the teams look for items to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: There is fallout from Friday night for Becky and Zara, while Theresa and Phil Sutton receive some bad news and Karen suspects one of Sid's patients of something(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an expat couple who want to return from Cyprus to buy a house in Lincolnshire, and also visits a college to find out about the next generation of farmers(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst go off on a search for items, setting out from the South Downs and heading towards an auction in Christchurch on the Dorset coast(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Angellica Bell host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out South
programme.desc: Jon Cuthill investigates a Gosport firm that has left owners of small businesses out of pocket and tied to long, expensive phone contracts. The programme also follows 17-year-old American football player Paul Fifield from Reading as he joins hundreds of youngsters hoping to be chosen for one of just 80 places at the NFL's new UK sports academy(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The events of last week continue to hang heavy in Walford. Chantelle passes her work assessment with flying colours but as the family celebrates Gray seems determined to sabotage the event. Tina is sorely tempted when Shirley tells her about a job she is doing for Phil abroad and asks her to come along(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Growing numbers of young people are carrying knives and becoming victims of knife crime, while doctors report that the injuries from knives they are treating in hospital are becoming more severe and the victims getting younger. In this programme, Chanell Wallace, whose own brother was stabbed to death, meets young people growing up in communities where carrying a knife is normal, sees how violence from knife crime is turning lives upside down and spends time in a school and college to see the impact of knives on the classroom(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: This second heat week sees five more celebrities enter the MasterChef kitchen. with Dom Parker, Josie Long, Mim Shaikh, Jenny Ryan and Alex George facing three culinary challenges. They first tackle an invention test, before they are split into two groups to take on the challenge of cooking for the first time in a restaurant kitchen, preparing dishes for paying customers. In the final task they are asked to prepare a dish of their own design in just one hour, before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace decide which of the contestants will leave the competition(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The vicar's son returns to the village and has to readjust to rural life having lived in Bristol, while Kerry tries to give back to the community by visiting an elderly neighbour whose cat has recently passed away(n)

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Footage of emergency services personnel assaulted in the line of duty. An arrest in Edinburgh escalates, resulting in an officer severing an artery. Meanwhile, PC Ian Bruce, who is part of a specialist team tackling vehicle crime, finds himself alone and vulnerable after members of a gang he is chasing turn on him(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Aussie comic Adam Hills hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by actress, comedienne and shoe-lover Andi Osho and funnyman-turned-EastEnder Terry Alderton(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley is live in Liverpool where police are working to tackle violent crime, while former detective Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK, as well as tips, advice and features(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a former hotel in Matlock, a piece of land in Penkhull, Stoke, and a terraced house in Selhurst(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Fly-tippers are caught on camera dumping 150 bags of waste in a forest in the Rhondda Valley, and a raid is made on a Lincoln address where dogs are kept for illegal fights(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A schoolboy relives the day he was injured while practicing BMX tricks, and a horse rider recalls the moment when she was thrown by her mount in the middle of an amateur race(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw presents from the Westpoint Arena in Exeter as experts Richard Madley and Charlie Ross help two teams to make the most profit at auction. Paul also visits the city's Met Office to learn about the history of weather forecasting(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma wants to know what is wrong with Zara, while Ruhma pays another visit to a newborn. Ayesha has her phone stolen, which leads her into a complex family situation(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman looks for a period property in rural West Sussex suitable for a couple with a toddler. She also tries her hand at the rare art of fore-edge painting(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a tranquil garden in Newport, Wales, which must be suitable for growing herbs and practicing meditation(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the New Forest and Southampton Water, and learn about tide milling. Items under the hammer include a mantle clock and a pocket watch(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany berates Bianca for not being there for her and Bobby is stunned when he overhears Rainie and Ian talking about their past. Gray feels increasingly left out by the Taylors and takes it out on Chantelle, while Jean lies about her operation being postponed to stop everyone fussing - but Daniel sees through her lies(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric celebrates his 65th birthday, but as he continues to make mistakes at work, the return of a face from the past forces him to realise that his surgical skills are fading. Fletch risks his chances of making peace with Ange as he tries to discover if Chloe's story is true, and Essie is forced to assume the role of a newly adoptive mother after Sacha jumps to the wrong conclusion(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The crucial CCTV footage of Shaun's supposed crime is seized by a mysterious MI5 officer, leaving Rachel convinced the truth behind the case is not as clear cut as it first appeared. However, getting answers may prove difficult when the surveillance system is found wanting. After a confrontation with his ex-partner, Shaun is determined to prove his innocence, and recruits an old friend to help him break into Hannah's flat and find evidence. Ron Perlman (Hellboy) joins the cast of the conspiracy thriller(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Terry gives Shauna one final warning when the police receive a report of drugs at her house. Along the coast, a brutal bar brawl leaves a man seriously injured - and the pub forced to close. In Dawdon, a man is suspected of stabbing a local resident, but proves difficult to track down after he goes into hiding - exposing deep-seated trust issues between the police and the townsfolk(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates: Extreme Russia
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Siberia with international model scouts in search of new recruits. In the area's capital of Novosibirsk, he explores how girls as young as five are training to join the industry by learning how to walk, pose, diet and apply make up. He also questions how likely the hopefuls are to succeed, investigates the pitfalls of the business and finds out what lives the successful youngsters are leaving behind(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Philadelphia Eagles as Super Bowl-winners(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter V, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost gives advice on how to prune overgrown evergreen shrubs and adds to his borders to create height. Rachel de Thame shows how to maintain containers and refresh with plants that will extend the display into autumn, and there's a feature on a gardener whose frequent absences from home have influenced the design and planting of his garden(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition marking 80 years since the first wave of evacuees were sent from towns and cities to the safety of the countryside, Steve Brown retraces his grandmother Rose's journey from Croydon to Cornwall. Sean Fletcher experiences life as an evacuee with schoolchildren at Acton Scott Historic Farm, while Margherita Taylor meets people who were relocated to Chatsworth House for safety during the war(n)

programme.title: The Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Carole Baxter looks at traditional lawn care and Brian Cunningham maintains his 'no-mow meadow'. Kirsty Wilson showcases indoor ideas to keep green fingers busy(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater: Eating Together
programme.desc: Nigel focuses on dishes that bring people together, heading to Trowbridge in Wiltshire to discover what draws Moroccan families around a traditional lamb tagine. He also gets a lesson in the art of pairing spices for an Indonesian fish dish and meets Zoe, who shows him how to make Ghanaian jollof rice. Inspired by their recipes, Nigel creates a Sunday roast of pork belly and shredded duck with spiced flatbread(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Producers making baked goods fight it out for a place in the final. Phoebe, a youth worker from London, makes free-from chocolate brownies. Bavarian-born Angelika now lives in Lancashire where she makes and sells pretzels. Tech company worker Mike bakes traditional sourdough in his spare time. Finally, Utako works in finance in London, but makes Japanese bread on the side(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: Rick's journey draws to a close as he heads east from Oaxaca to the Yucatan Peninsula, where dishes like Papadzules and Sikil Pak are enjoyed in villages across the countryside. Here, the locals have lighter skins and blue-green eyes, and feast of the hottest chilli of them all, the Habanero. Rick ends his journey feasting on grilled seafood in Tulum, along the shores of the Caribbean Sea(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes journalist and TV presenter Angela Rippon back to Mount Edgcumbe Country Park in Cornwall, where she reminisces about the holiday she had there in 1952. The Strictly Come Dancing judge surprises her by arranging a trip on a vintage bus just like the one she would have taken to her accommodation at the time, which was a camp consisting of a series of converted Nissen huts that had been used by American soldiers during the war(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where Christina Trevanion and David Fletcher assess items including a large collection of boxed Corgi toys and some rare watches. Meanwhile, Paul Martin discovers the dramatic political life of one of Sandon's earlier owners, who was a member of William Pitt's cabinet in tumultuous times(n)

programme.title: Equestrian
programme.desc: Action from the competition in Stamford, Lincolnshire, a prestigious three-day eventing competition that forms part of the Rolex Grand Slam(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the half-marathon that ran from Newcastle upon Tyne to South Shields, as Mo Farah and Vivian Cheruiyot aimed to successfully defend their respective elite men's and women's titles, while tens of thousands of amateur runners raised money for charity(n)

programme.title: The Ganges with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Sue begins her final leg in the ancient city of Patna, which was once the centre of the British Empire's lucrative opium trade but is now known for education. In Kolkata, she is reunited with Rakhi, one of the street kids who made such an impression on the presenter during a previous visit two years earlier, then finds out what it takes to protect one of the world's most endangered cats - the Bengal tiger. Finally she reaches Saga Island in the mouth of the river, where from dusk till dawn two million people are expected to bathe in the holy river(n)

programme.title: Grizzly Bear Cubs and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. With only 10 weeks before they are due to be released, Gordon Buchanan begins training the cubs for their new life in the Russian wilderness. They learn survival skills and spend time exploring a special wilderness training zone just outside the enclosure where they can experience the wild. However, little cub Pasha is still struggling, and the team worry he may not make it(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: The panel oversee more business propositions, with returning Dragon Theo Paphitis joining regulars Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies. Two entrepreneurs seeking funding for a range of drinks go to the lengths of setting up a bar in the studio, while other items up for assessment include a design for a pipe-fitting tool and a range of men's cosmetics. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the political influence of billionaire George Soros, who has been accused by conspiracy theorists and politicians alike of attempting to sway elections and control immigration policies. The programme questions how many of these stories have basis in fact, and how many are grounded in antisemitism(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: After reflecting on the fallout of the first week, Louise discovers something troubling about Tom. Rosamund Pike and Chris O'Dowd star in a comedy, written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas v Homophobia: The Legacy
programme.desc: The former rugby international continues his campaign to wipe out homophobia in football. With reported incidents of homophobic abuse at football matches at a record high, Gareth enlists the help of Parliament as he attempts to outlaw abuse in stadia. He also recruits Cardiff City, Leeds United and Norwich City in his campaign to tackle homophobia in the boardroom, the dressing room and on the terraces. All of this while coping with being the victim of a violent homophobic attack himself(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The contestants are split into pairs and challenged to transform shop interiors, revamping a series of independent stores on a high street in Nottingham in just two days. In a vintage store, the designers go right back to basics, stripping the very high walls and ceiling, while frustrations mount in a women's clothing store as last-minute changes are made to the design plans. With the shop makeovers complete, Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Mary Portas decide who has impressed, and who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brothers help Elizabeth from Brighton to turn a paved-over blank square of land into a city garden full of colour and plants, on a healthy budget of £6,000(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers in Pembrokeshire, including brothers Hal, Will and Ben, race to the scene when a plane makes an emergency crash landing on one of Wales' busiest beaches. In Jersey, the St Catherine crew are pushed to the brink when they search for a man who has gone missing at sea in the middle of a fierce winter storm. On the Wirral, the crew of Hoylake launch their specialist rescue hovercraft and race the incoming tide to try and save a horse, which has become stuck fast in the mud and at risk from drowning(n)

programme.title: This Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Hermione Cockburn discovers the secrets to the success of British seaside towns on a visit to Blackpool's Pleasure Beach, and looks back at Rhyl's glory years through Victorian postcards. Neil Oliver also finds out about the people who manned Liverpool's dockyards(n)

programme.title: The Life and Death of Colonel Blimp (1943)
programme.desc: A Home Guard officer serving during the Second World War is outraged by the disrespect for tradition shown by younger soldiers. He looks back on his military adventures during the Boer War and First World War, as well as his three great loves. Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger's satirical drama, starring Roger Livesey, Deborah Kerr, Anton Walbrook and John Laurie(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television and film appearances made over the years by actress Deborah Kerr, capturing the milestones and highlights of her life and career that led to her being considered a Hollywood icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Grand Pier at Weston-super-Mare in Somerset, where experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt pick out some gems to value. Among them are rare medals that once belonged to an early aviator and puppets that look like the Swedish band Abba. Plus, Paul delves into the history of seaside entertainment.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: Head of birds Lorraine and vet Romain join forces when a juvenile raven with a broken wing requires a groundbreaking operation if it is to have a chance of flying again(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling an old Salford tram seat, a vintage horse-racing game, two stained-glass panels, and a mirror box with a theatrical past(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as Rome enthusiasts the Gladiators take on wordsmiths the Lexplorers. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Don Quixote, Pandarus, Gargantua and Mrs Malaprop(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The eighth first-round match sees four students from Durham University pit their wits against a team from Trinity College, Cambridge in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: It is 1933 and Adolf Hitler is the chancellor of Germany, but he does not have absolute power as there is still a democratic parliament, a head of state and the rule of law. Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation, but a fire in the Reichstag building is blamed on communists - prompting thousands of arrests of left-wingers. Hitler then calls on President von Hindenburg to act and a decree is passed giving the Nazis emergency powers to ban free speech and the right to protest(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Mark Owen reflects on the year his band Take That took the nation by storm and Gladiators became essential Saturday-night viewing, bringing characters such as Wolf and Jet to the screen. Wayne's World's spoof homage to rock was a box-office hit, while violent thriller Reservoir Dogs shot director Quentin Tarantino into the limelight. On the musical front, the Shamen's Ebeneezer Goode brought dance music to the charts _ and caused a storm of controversy over its alleged Ecstasy-promoting lyrics(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Golf: Walker Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the 47th staging of the amateur team competition between Great Britain & Ireland and USA, held this year at Royal Liverpool Golf Club in Hoylake(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The rehearsals for Chicago are finally under way, having been delayed by Fosse's hospitalisation for a heart attack, but his insecurities soon come to the fore. Bob is hard on Gwen in rehearsals and suggests she is having problems keeping up with the younger cast, while she is concerned about the ending of the show and asks for a new song(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: As the town is engulfed by a storm, Jac faces a devastating confrontation with her old rival Faye Byrne and the man whom they both loved. Sacha helps a grieving family that is torn over the issue of organ donation to come to a difficult decision. Patsy Kensit reprises her role as Faye for the first time since 2010(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition marking 80 years since the first wave of evacuees were sent from towns and cities to the safety of the countryside, Steve Brown retraces his grandmother Rose's journey from Croydon to Cornwall. Sean Fletcher experiences life as an evacuee with schoolchildren at Acton Scott Historic Farm, while Margherita Taylor meets people who were relocated to Chatsworth House for safety during the war(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst go off on a search for items, setting out from the South Downs and heading towards an auction in Christchurch on the Dorset coast(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers work to keep the roads safe after four people are killed in a road accident when a lorry driver using his mobile phone crashes into them(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the penultimate round of fixtures, including Hull Kingston Rovers v London Broncos and St Helens v Huddersfield Giants(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Families from Mersea Island, Essex, and Morecambe, Lancashire, put their homes and lifestyles under the microscope as they exchange properties. After the swap, they reveal whether or not they enjoyed the experience(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A tip-off about an empty house prompts a search for Elizabeth Dean's heirs, while Michael Buerk and the team reveal a colonial link to slavery in Henry Arthur Coke's case(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: The construction of HMS Astute, the Royal Navy's biggest and most powerful attack submarine, which cost more than £1billion and is able to circumnavigate the globe without surfacing. The programme follows engineers, designers and skilled craftsmen as they work on the vessel, from the initial stages of production to its first journey into the open sea(n)

programme.title: Shark
programme.desc: A look at how scientists are challenging traditional perceptions of sharks, with breakthroughs regarding the creatures' intelligence and social skills presenting them in a surprising new light. The programme also highlights some researchers' beliefs that studying the predators could help to solve some of the most pressing medical challenges of the 21st century. Narrated by Paul McGann(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Norwich Cathedral, where Thomas Plant and Kate Bateman unearth items including a religious relic and a Martin Brothers vase, and Paul Martin visits the circus(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz show in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: Staff at the SSPCA's wildlife rescue centre take care of two white-tailed eagles and have to use all their experience and expertise to try to get them back in the wild(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a threadbare theatre chair from Derby Hippodrome, a rare timepiece that has not worked for years, a cherished bronze locket and two First World War flying mascots(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the east coast of Scotland, volunteers from Dunbar race to save three children trapped in a cave by rising tides, while in the west, the Tobermory crew comes to the aid of a yacht caught in a storm and being forced onto the rocks. In England, the Blyth crew searches for an 80-year-old man who has gone missing after leaving home to look for lobsters on the coast(n)

programme.title: Seahorse: The Dad Who Gave Birth (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about a gay transgender man's efforts to have a baby, following his story over the course of three years from the initial struggle to conceive to the eventual birth, and examining the challenges and prejudices he has faced, forcing him to rely on the support of friends and family in the face of mounting hostility(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the opening round of fixtures, including New England Patriots v Pittsburgh Steelers(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: The BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra celebrates conductor Martyn Brabbins's 60th birthday in music by Vaughan Williams, Brahms and Elgar's Enigma Variations, from the Royal Albert Hall. Presented by Suzy Klein and Anna Meredith. Elgar's Enigma Variations is the inspiration for a new work commissioned from 14 living composers as a special birthday tribute to conductor Martyn Brabbins. Elgar's original set also features, as do Vaughan Williams's exquisite Serenade to Music and Brahms' Little Requiem, the Song of Destiny(n)

programme.title: Jerusalem: An Anthem for England
programme.desc: Lovers of the hymn, including members of the Women's Institute and politicians, explain the appeal it holds for them, and give their opinions in the debate about whether Jerusalem should become England's official national anthem. Featuring contributions by Roy Hattersley, Ann Widdecombe and Gary Bushell(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Professor Chris Lintott visits the UK Astronomy Technology Centre in Edinburgh, to see a camera designed to take pictures of exoplanets, and journeys through exoplanet discoveries(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the achievements and discoveries of the Cassini space probe mission, which changed the way scientists regard the solar system. Its 13 years spent orbiting Saturn brought revelations of gigantic ice geysers, hidden underground oceans that could harbour life and a brand new moon coalescing in the planet's rings. Narrated by Paterson Joseph(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary following citizens of the West Virginia town of Parkersburg as they take on powerful corporation DuPont after discovering it has knowingly been dumping a toxic chemical into their water supply - one which is now found in the blood of 99.7 per cent of Americans(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC: The Genius of Leonardo da Vinci
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez explores the BBC archives to explore the work of the Renaissance artist, revealing what lies beneath the Mona Lisa and how Da Vinci acquired his anatomical knowledge(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the beauty of butterflies close up, and revealing how they inspire the images of street artist Nick Walker and the performances of burlesque dancer Vicky Butterfly. The programme also raises the question of whether a passion for these elegant insects could help people preserve much-loved landscapes. Narrated by Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom's Year with the Birds
programme.desc: As spring makes way for summer in Exmoor, wildlife expert Johnny Kingdom is relieved to discover a healthy brood of wren chicks and finds that some of the adults survived the harsh winter. He is also pleased with his footage of young swallows, and returns to one of his old hides in a bid to film the offspring of a pair of barn owls, as well as enlisting the help of an old friend to record the behaviour of woodpecker chicks(n)

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore reveals how Rome cast aside its pantheon of pagan gods, and Emperor Constantine took a huge leap of faith by promoting Christianity as the religion of the empire. He charts the rocky course of Rome's rise to become the capital of Western Christendom, and its impact on the lives of its citizens, elites and high priests(n)

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Jodhpur to Delhi, heading into the desert to take a camel ride to a village where life has changed little in centuries, before embarking on the Jaipur-Agra-Delhi 'Golden Triangle' tour - India's must-do itinerary in 1913 as today. Michael also visits Agra, where he learns how the Taj Mahal, built by a Mughal emperor, was restored by a British viceroy at the time of his Bradshaw's guide book(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Saul David uses the BBC archive to chart the history of the world's most destructive war, looking at how the story of the conflict has changed as new information comes to light and forgotten stories are remembered. He examines the most important accounts of the Second World War, and how the understanding of them has been redefined since the conflict ended(n)

programme.title: Fighting for King and Empire: Britain's Caribbean Heroes
programme.desc: During the Second World War, thousands of men and women from Britain's Caribbean colonies volunteered to join the fight against Hitler, but their contribution has largely been forgotten. In this documentary based on the film Divided By Race - United in War and Peace, veterans tell their extraordinary wartime stories and reveal how they have faced a lifelong struggle to be treated as equals by the UK government and the British people(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes' series profiling the most celebrated thinkers of the Ancient world continues as she turns her attention to Socrates. Heading to Greece, she details how the Athenian philosopher secured a reputation as an influential maverick. She also highlights how his contributions as one of the founders of Western philosophy did not please his detractors, as his outspoken defence of his beliefs ultimately led to his execution(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: The history of the coach, which offered a cheaper alternative to rail travel upon its arrival in the 1950s and gave greater freedom to people previously restricted to their own villages and towns due to limited funds. The documentary looks at the distinctive liveries employed by different transport companies and the activities of passengers, including singing and pub visits, as they travelled to destinations such as Blackpool, Margate and Torquay. Narrated by June Whitfield(n)

programme.title: Metalworks!
programme.desc: Tobias Capwell explores how Henry VIII combined German technology with Renaissance artistry in his quest to conform to the image of the perfect knight. He also reveals how the monarch's daughter, Elizabeth I, exploited the image further by making her courtiers parade before her in the most innovative and richly decorated suits of armour ever commissioned in steel(n)

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore reveals how Rome cast aside its pantheon of pagan gods, and Emperor Constantine took a huge leap of faith by promoting Christianity as the religion of the empire. He charts the rocky course of Rome's rise to become the capital of Western Christendom, and its impact on the lives of its citizens, elites and high priests(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Great Irish Journeys with Martha Kearney
programme.desc: The broadcaster follows in the footsteps of 19th-century artist George Victor Du Noyer, who spent 35 years charting the landscape, people and buildings of Ireland. In the first episode, Martha gives an introduction to Du Noyer's work, and travels to the Bog of Allen, which lies between the Liffey and Shannon, and the Old Head of Kinsale in Co Cork(n)

programme.title: Beyond the Walls: In Search of the Celts
programme.desc: Historian Dr Eleanor Barraclough travels through some of Britain's most beautiful landscapes - Hadrian's Wall, the Lake District and Offa's Dyke - in search of new evidence to reveal the true story of the mysterious ancient British tribes often called the Celts(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Documentary unearthing new evidence on the beginnings of the Troubles. The first episode focuses on unrest between unionists and nationalists in the 1960s and the arrival of the British army towards the decade's end, and the downfall of the Northern Ireland government in 1972, when IRA members believed they were about to force the British Army out of Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Squaddies on the Frontline
programme.desc: The story of the British Army's experience of the Northern Ireland conflict between 1969 and 2007, told through the eyes of the men and women that were stationed there, who were at the heart of the key events throughout the conflict(n)

programme.title: Steve Jobs: Billion Dollar Hippy
programme.desc: Evan Davis traces the history of Steve Jobs' time in control at Apple, with contributions from insiders including the company's co-founder Steve Wozniak, world wide web inventor Tim Berners-Lee, branding guru Rita Clifton and Stephen Fry. The presenter hears stories of how Jobs, a college dropout, was involved in the company's rise from operating out of a suburban garage to becoming a global brand(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage of the world's biggest half-marathon, which sees tens of thousands of runners of all abilities negotiate the course from Newcastle upon Tyne to South Shields. British favourite Mo Farah won the men's elite race for a record fifth time in 2018, beating New Zealand's Jake Robertson into second place as he did 12 months earlier. A home favourite also prevailed in the men's wheelchair race, with David Weir claiming his seventh title here, while Kenyan Vivian Cheruiyot won the elite women's event for the second time in three years and Poland's Martyna Snopek was the women's wheelchair champion(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores sacred architecture 350 years after Christopher Wren started work on St Paul's Cathedral. Josie d'Arby meets Christian artists collective Morphe Arts(n)

programme.title: Points of View

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore reclaims three items from a Walsall recycling centre, with designer Rupert Blanchard given the task of bringing a boring bookcase into the 21st century(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In south Wales, Kate and Teg set a mountain alight and experience the heat of a controlled burn with the local fire service in an area with the highest incidence of wild fires in the whole of the UK. Kate gets the creative juices flowing by lending a hand to the creation of an epic land art piece - a 30ft mandala - a stunning geometrically patterned circle made entirely from natural materials. Teg gets a run for her money, when meeting film actors at a rural Film Academy for Animals, and Kate fulfils her childhood dream with a master farrier, learning how to shoe a horse(n)

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens (2009)
programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: Solo climber Eric Jones embarks on one final mountain ascent at the age of 82 - journeying to the south-west ridge of the Torre Delago in the Italian Dolomites. Eric reflects on his own life and not just his climbing achievements, and explains how he fell in love with a young Australian teacher who abandoned her homeland for a new life in Snowdonia(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Kelly Cates, Adrian Chiles, Andy Hamilton, Kate Williams, Mark Dolan, Rustie Lee, Esther Rantzen and Tim Vine(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Northumberland, where Helen Skelton visits a scheme in Blyth in which students from disadvantaged areas work with retired engineers and craftsmen to learn traditional boat-building and shipwright skills. Adam Henson meets the next contestant hoping to lift the trophy in this year's One Man and His Dog competition and Matt Baker has all the information on how viewers can take part in this year's Countryfile Ramble for Children in Need(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Dover Castle for a special edition marking 80 years since the start of the Second World War, bringing insights into the conflict through personal stories and family mementos. A photo album belonging to an Austrian soldier offers a previously unseen glimpse of Neville Chamberlain's ill-fated meeting with Adolf Hitler in 1938, while Fiona Bruce meets 95-year-old Ray Palmer, who has the rare distinction of being both a child evacuee and then a serving soldier(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy waits by the water at Charlie's Yard to meet a dangerous adversary, but despite their past grievances the pair agree to a truce. Tommy receives a message from a mysterious stranger during his constituency surgery, while at his Westminster office, the MP makes Aberama a very tempting offer, and later invites Mosley to a party he is throwing for Lizzie's birthday. On the night of the event, things soon take a turn for the worse when a surprise guest arrives. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers check hygiene standards in Oxford takeaways while in Studley, Warwickshire, they crack down on a butcher claiming meat was British when it came from overseas(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Growing numbers of young people are carrying knives and becoming victims of knife crime, while doctors report that the injuries from knives they are treating in hospital are becoming more severe and the victims getting younger. In this programme, Chanell Wallace, whose own brother was stabbed to death, meets young people growing up in communities where carrying a knife is normal, sees how violence from knife crime is turning lives upside down and spends time in a school and college to see the impact of knives on the classroom(n)

programme.desc: This second heat week sees five more celebrities enter the MasterChef kitchen. with Dom Parker, Josie Long, Mim Shaikh, Jenny Ryan and Alex George facing three culinary challenges. They first tackle an invention test, before they are split into two groups to take on the challenge of cooking for the first time in a restaurant kitchen, preparing dishes for paying customers. In the final task they are asked to prepare a dish of their own design in just one hour, before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace decide which of the contestants will leave the competition(n)

programme.desc: The vicar's son returns to the village and has to readjust to rural life having lived in Bristol, while Kerry tries to give back to the community by visiting an elderly neighbour whose cat has recently passed away(n)

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Footage of emergency services personnel assaulted in the line of duty. An arrest in Edinburgh escalates, resulting in an officer severing an artery. Gray feels increasingly left out by the Taylors and takes it out on Chantelle, while Jean lies about her operation being postponed to stop everyone fussing - but Daniel sees through her lies(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric celebrates his 65th birthday, but as he continues to make mistakes at work, the return of a face from the past forces him to realise that his surgical skills are fading. programme.desc: The crucial CCTV footage of Shaun's supposed crime is seized by a mysterious MI5 officer, leaving Rachel convinced the truth behind the case is not as clear cut as it first appeared. However, getting answers may prove difficult when the surveillance system is found wanting. After a confrontation with his ex-partner, Shaun is determined to prove his innocence, and recruits an old friend to help him break into Hannah's flat and find evidence. Ron Perlman (Hellboy) joins the cast of the conspiracy thriller(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates: Extreme Russia

He also questions how likely the hopefuls are to succeed, investigates the pitfalls of the business and finds out what lives the successful youngsters are leaving behind(n)

programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Northumberland, where Helen Skelton visits a scheme in Blyth in which students from disadvantaged areas work with retired engineers and craftsmen to learn traditional boat-building and shipwright skills. Adam Henson meets the next contestant hoping to lift the trophy in this year's One Man and His Dog competition and Matt Baker has all the information on how viewers can take part in this year's Countryfile Ramble for Children in Need(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Dover Castle for a special edition marking 80 years since the start of the Second World War, bringing insights into the conflict through personal stories and family mementos. A photo album belonging to an Austrian soldier offers a previously unseen glimpse of Neville Chamberlain's ill-fated meeting with Adolf Hitler in 1938, while Fiona Bruce meets 95-year-old Ray Palmer, who has the rare distinction of being both a child evacuee and then a serving soldier(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy waits by the water at Charlie's Yard to meet a dangerous adversary, but despite their past grievances the pair agree to a truce. Tommy receives a message from a mysterious stranger during his constituency surgery, while at his Westminster office, the MP makes Aberama a very tempting offer, and later invites Mosley to a party he is throwing for Lizzie's birthday. On the night of the event, things soon take a turn for the worse when a surprise guest arrives. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United(n)

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits houses in Stourbridge and Liverpool, and there is a look at a Grade II- listed property in Brentwood, Essex, that Martin Roberts first saw back in 2016(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst go off on a search for items, setting out from the South Downs and heading towards an auction in Christchurch on the Dorset coast(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Angellica Bell host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels around the firths of Easter Ross and Sutherland, beginning at Loch Ussie - famous for its connections with the Brahan Seer, who predicted future calamities. On a golf course above Strathpeffer, he discovers an unlikely connection between the conventional old spa town below and occultist and author Aleister Crowley, before heading for the Dornoch Firth, where he encounters a witch and a fairy bridge(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The events of last week continue to hang heavy in Walford. Chantelle passes her work assessment with flying colours but as the family celebrates Gray seems determined to sabotage the event. Tina is sorely tempted when Shirley tells her about a job she is doing for Phil abroad and asks her to come along(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Growing numbers of young people are carrying knives and becoming victims of knife crime, while doctors report that the injuries from knives they are treating in hospital are becoming more severe and the victims getting younger. In this programme, Chanell Wallace, whose own brother was stabbed to death, meets young people growing up in communities where carrying a knife is normal, sees how violence from knife crime is turning lives upside down and spends time in a school and college to see the impact of knives on the classroom(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: This second heat week sees five more celebrities enter the MasterChef kitchen. with Dom Parker, Josie Long, Mim Shaikh, Jenny Ryan and Alex George facing three culinary challenges. They first tackle an invention test, before they are split into two groups to take on the challenge of cooking for the first time in a restaurant kitchen, preparing dishes for paying customers. In the final task they are asked to prepare a dish of their own design in just one hour, before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace decide which of the contestants will leave the competition(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The vicar's son returns to the village and has to readjust to rural life having lived in Bristol, while Kerry tries to give back to the community by visiting an elderly neighbour whose cat has recently passed away(n)

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Footage of emergency services personnel assaulted in the line of duty. An arrest in Edinburgh escalates, resulting in an officer severing an artery. Meanwhile, PC Ian Bruce, who is part of a specialist team tackling vehicle crime, finds himself alone and vulnerable after members of a gang he is chasing turn on him(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Aussie comic Adam Hills hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by actress, comedienne and shoe-lover Andi Osho and funnyman-turned-EastEnder Terry Alderton(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a tranquil garden in Newport, Wales, which must be suitable for growing herbs and practicing meditation(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany berates Bianca for not being there for her and Bobby is stunned when he overhears Rainie and Ian talking about their past. Gray feels increasingly left out by the Taylors and takes it out on Chantelle, while Jean lies about her operation being postponed to stop everyone fussing - but Daniel sees through her lies(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Dougie's mental state unravels after he finds about about Scarlett's lies, while Joe tries to find a way out of the corner he has been backed into, and Nicole must try anything to keep Alex's interest(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The crucial CCTV footage of Shaun's supposed crime is seized by a mysterious MI5 officer, leaving Rachel convinced the truth behind the case is not as clear cut as it first appeared. However, getting answers may prove difficult when the surveillance system is found wanting. After a confrontation with his ex-partner, Shaun is determined to prove his innocence, and recruits an old friend to help him break into Hannah's flat and find evidence. Ron Perlman (Hellboy) joins the cast of the conspiracy thriller(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric celebrates his 65th birthday, but as he continues to make mistakes at work, the return of a face from the past forces him to realise that his surgical skills are fading. Fletch risks his chances of making peace with Ange as he tries to discover if Chloe's story is true, and Essie is forced to assume the role of a newly adoptive mother after Sacha jumps to the wrong conclusion(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Terry gives Shauna one final warning when the police receive a report of drugs at her house. Along the coast, a brutal bar brawl leaves a man seriously injured - and the pub forced to close. In Dawdon, a man is suspected of stabbing a local resident, but proves difficult to track down after he goes into hiding - exposing deep-seated trust issues between the police and the townsfolk(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Victoria Fritz present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage of the world's biggest half-marathon, which sees tens of thousands of runners of all abilities negotiate the course from Newcastle upon Tyne to South Shields. British favourite Mo Farah won the men's elite race for a record fifth time in 2018, beating New Zealand's Jake Robertson into second place as he did 12 months earlier. A home favourite also prevailed in the men's wheelchair race, with David Weir claiming his seventh title here, while Kenyan Vivian Cheruiyot won the elite women's event for the second time in three years and Poland's Martyna Snopek was the women's wheelchair champion(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores sacred architecture 350 years after Christopher Wren started work on St Paul's Cathedral. Josie d'Arby meets Christian artists collective Morphe Arts(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Removals men are busy at Phil's house as he and Edith make plans for their wedding. The one thing they are sure about is that Bob will be excluded from the event. Roger tells Edith how he will miss her and then announces that he is in love with Georgie, Bob's daughter. Edith explodes at his lack of judgement, but after swiftly changing the subject, Roger gets her agreement for a wedding video, which he says will be his present to her and Phil. However, he then proceeds to put Georgie behind the camera. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an active couple return to their roots in Shropshire, looking for a property within their £350,000 budget that gives them the great outdoors on their doorstep(n)

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens (2009)
programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore reclaims three items from a Walsall recycling centre, with designer Rupert Blanchard given the task of bringing a boring bookcase into the 21st century(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Documentary unearthing new evidence on the beginnings of the Troubles. programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Eighty years ago, hundreds of Jewish children were smuggled out of Nazi occupied Europe by train in a covert humanitarian mission which became known as the ‘kindertransport'. Stephen Sackur speaks to Dame Stephanie Shirley, who was one of those children. She went on to live an extraordinary life of achievement and philanthropy, blazing a trail for women in business, science and technology. What lessons can we learn from a woman determined to make the most of a life so nearly extinguished in childhood?(n)

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: The Scottish Government-led debate in the Scottish Parliament on avoiding a no-deal exit from the EU, from Thursday 5 September.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts the return of the political debate from Westminster. The panellists are Brexit minister Kwasi Kwarteng, Shadow Foreign Secretary Emily Thornberry, Lib Dem Digital, Culture, Media and Sport spokesperson Layla Moran, Leader of the SNP at Westminster Ian Blackford, Brexit Party chairman Richard Tice and radio host Iain Dale(n)

programme.desc: Coverage of Exiting the European Union Committee's session on the progress of the UK's negotiations on EU withdrawal with evidence from Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster Michael Gove(n)

programme.title: Select Committees

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Tthe challenges of winter on the croft as Sweeny contends with 2018's infamous Beast from the East and some really cold weather. As a full time crofter, Sweeny is also constantly exploring new ideas to ensure a steady income throughout the year. One of these new ideas leads him to a new hobby - knitting - as he heads to the Isle of Skye to get creative with his wool(n)

programme.title: Druthag na Hearadh (A Little Drop of Harris)
programme.desc: Seven years in the planning, two years in its construction, the Harris Distillery is in its first year of existence. This first of three episodes follows the lead up to and first working year at the Harris Distillery. After some delays in construction and the inevitable technical glitches, their Harris Gin goes straight into production. Hot on its heels is the distillery's primary function, and the team works up to distilling its first Hearach whisky. The distillery staff have all been recruited locally and are collectively known as the 'Tarbert Ten'. They share a commonality in having never been involved in the distillery business, but are keen to prove that they are more than able to create a historic happening on the Isle of Harris(n)

programme.title: Clann Pheter Roraidh
programme.desc: The story of the Campbell family from the Isle of Barra. Tony Kearney is a well-known Scottish actor and broadcaster, but his family's talents extend back generations. programme.title: The Flatey Enigma
programme.desc: Icelandic drama set in 1971, following a professor of Nordic Studies who is accused of murder. To prove her innocence, she must solve a riddle in a medieval manuscript about Norse kings, the Flatey Enigma. programme.desc: Spy-themed game show in which two friends work together to foil the plans of evil mastermind Goldfist(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prominent resident is robbed and battered to death with her walking stick, and suspicion soon falls on the members of her reading club - which turns out to be a front for a lucrative investment portfolio. John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born)(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh prepares a surprise for Rob and Margaret Isdale, a Grantham couple in their seventies who have fostered more than 150 children over the past 45 years. Alan draws inspiration from Frances Hodgson Burnett's novel The Secret Garden, creating ancient looking follies and winding paths, and even turning a normal garden shed into a mock-Tudor feature(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the spin-off from the talent show in which winners and memorable acts from past series return to the fray, competing against each other and performers from Got Talent shows from around the world. The second installment features the return of Lost Voice Guy, the first comedian to ever win the show, the group Mersey Girls from series 11, and series 5's winner Jai McDowall. Joining the British entries is an unusual act from Germany - Alexa Lauenburger and her eight performing dogs(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Comedian Tim Vine, co-host of The Great British Bake Off Mel Giedroyc and former Pussycat Doll Ashley Roberts line up to 'see it and say it'. Competing to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues and win money for charity, one of them receives the chance to bag £50,000 for a good cause. Stephen Mulhern hosts(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites former Pussycat Doll Kimberly Wyatt, Emmerdale's Duncan Preston (Doug Potts) and reality star Bobby Cole Norris to take part in the quiz show, answering questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity(n)

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: A pair of exes and a father-and-daughter duo take part in the game show, competing to answer visual questions by placing rings on an LED floor in five increasingly difficult stages, with a potential jackpot of more than £50,000 up for grabs. Hosted by Phillip Schofield(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Charlotte sets out to avoid Sidney, until a serious accident forces them together and to reappraise each other. When Tom brings a new doctor to the town, he and Lady Denham clash, while Clara makes a bold move in her bid to outplay Sir Edward and Esther. Drama adapted from the unfinished novel by Jane Austen, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Anne Reid, Charlotte Spencer, Kris Marshall and Kate Ashfield(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: The comedian embarks on a journey across the US, following the migratory trail of the Scots through America - starting in New York and finishing in Nashville, Tennessee. The first episode sees Billy open the New York Tartan Day parade in front of a 30,000-strong crowd. He then heads to Plymouth, Massachusetts, where the pilgrims first landed on American soil, followed by a meeting with the Native American tribe who almost had their people and language wiped out(n)

programme.title: The Best Little Prison in Britain?
programme.desc: Head of Security Margo is determined to get to the bottom of a argument which started over some boiled eggs, and the incident inspires one of the inmates involved to write a poem. Meanwhile, prisoner Adie is tasked with the unpleasant job of cleaning up a 'dirty protest', where a prisoner has used his own excrement to write on the walls(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: When 22-year-old Sian O'Callaghan goes missing after a Friday night out in Swindon, Detective Superintendent Stephen Fulcher knows that he and his team must move fast to have any chance of finding her alive. While Sian's family desperately wait for news, the police search for any clues as to where she could be. Fact-based crime drama, starring Martin Freeman, Imelda Staunton and Siobhan Finneran(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
