programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sinead tells Daniel that she's found a lump, and Ray invites Michelle up to his room to discuss the franchise plan, with alarming results. Meanwhile, Abi lets Kevin down gently, and Maria accuses Ali of cheating on her with Emma(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: The hunt for Sian O'Callaghan continues as Det Supt Steve Fulcher and his team see suspects come and go with no leads to follow. Local woman Karen Edwards notices that she only lives a few doors away from the missing girl's mother, Elaine Pickford, but is only reminded of her own problems, having not seen her daughter Becky for several years. With things looking bleak for Wiltshire police, a brilliant piece of police work finally points Fulcher towards a definite suspect. Fact-based crime drama, starring Martin Freeman(n)

programme.title: Fighter Pilot: The Real Top Gun
programme.desc: The three students face the final aerial combat and mission tests to decide if they are ready to take to the skies in the new F35 Lightning fighter jet. The squadron they hope to join returns to Britain from training in South Carolina, and junior pilot Liam must master one of the most difficult manoeuvres he will ever have to face - mid air refuelling(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: On the school trip, Lisa struggles to get close enough to Kate to ask her what has been going on, and watches in shock as Kate continues to play the role of the tragic mother of a missing daughter. The tension amidst the parents begins to rub off on the children, and a playful accident sees Fergus rushed into hospital. Lisa follows Kate and Fergus to hospital, determined to uncover the truth. Later, Roz is forced to tell Abby that Winston has moved out. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Kosovo (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the Group A qualifier at St Mary's Stadium in Southampton. Kosovo only played their first official match as an independent nation in 2014 and won just one of their eight matches when attempting to qualify for the 2018 World Cup. However they have demonstrated considerable signs of improvement in the current campaign, with the highlights so far a 3-2 win in Bulgaria, which suggests Gareth Southgate's men cannot take them lightly despite being the overwhelming favourites to win the group. With analysis from Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Lee Dixon, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Kosovo. Mark Pougatch presents action from the Group A qualifier at St Mary's Stadium in Southampton. Plus, a round-up of the rest of this evening's matches, including Lithuania v Portugal and France v Andorra. With analysis from Lee Dixon and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest (2006)

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End (2007)

programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamy's fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps embarks on a mission to make Aidensfield sparkle with his own brand of cleaning products. Drama, starring Aislin McGuckin and Duncan Bell(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: Joanna begins a 2,000-mile journey across Japan in Hokkaido, where she meets one of the most important animals in Japanese culture, the red-crowned crane. She arrives in Sapporo during the middle of the annual Snow Festival and meets members of the local indigenous community, before travelling into the Fukushima exclusion zone and taking a bullet train from Nagano to Tokyo(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: In the wake of the fall of France to the Germans, guests flood into the hotel, including one who catches Lady Hamilton's eye - the Comte de St Claire. Peggy worries about Billy's enthusiasm to sign up as soon as he turns 18, but knows there is little she can do to protect him when his mind looks like it's made up to join his father's regiment, infamously used as canon fodder. When Max faces hostility from the staff, Emma goes out on a limb to defend him - and immediately regrets it(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Joe is offered the opportunity of a lifetime, but finds himself unexpectedly torn as the moral responsibility of staying in England to tell the story of the war weighs heavily on his shoulders. Betsey's mother Gloria comes for a surprise stay, leaving Sonny unsettled and wary of the reaction she incites from Betsey, while Lucian and Lady Hamilton continue their flirtation and Emma anxiously awaits news of Freddie when she hears that a plane from Squadron 392 has gone down(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun
programme.desc: Carter returns to the Valley of the Kings as the First World War draws to a close, hoping to complete his search for the lost tomb. Maggie also arrives in Egypt hoping to resume their relationship, but Carter finds himself smitten with Lord Carnarvon's daughter Evelyn. The excavation is threatened by a tide of Anti-British sentiment sweeping across Egypt, and Carter and Evelyn get caught up in a bombing. Meanwhile, Carnarvon is in financial difficulties, and cannot afford to fund Carter's seemingly hopeless search(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A father is aghast to read tales of wild romance in his teenage daughter's diary, failing to realise they are all fictitious. David inherits a ventriloquist's dummy complete with a schedule of bookings, and decides the show must go on. Meanwhile, Gina finds herself attracted to a charismatic guest. Nostalgic drama, starring David Lonsdale and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank has to go to Edinburgh, so Kim plans a day Christmas shopping with Neil. Carol is amazed when Archie offers to buy her a drink, and Annie is taken ill(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of the Home Farm shoot arrives, Annie is in bed with bronchitis, Nick gets the wrong idea about Archie and Julie, and Mark feels terrible after a night in the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve refuses to have Jim in court with him, Alf throws a farewell party at the Rovers, and Don explains to Jack that he only shares the house with Ivy(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Brendan takes over the shop and renames it Best Buys, Vera is horrified when he tells her he inherited her debts, and Jack is enjoying living apart from Vera and her nagging(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Neville Chamberlain visits Downton for dinner, and is drawn into Violet's plan to save the hospital, with distressing consequences for everyone. Andy finds an unexpected confidante while helping Mr Mason move into Yew Tree Farm, Mary is uncertain if she has met her match, Edith meets Bertie Pelham in London, and Spratt comes to Denker's aid. Mrs Hughes struggles to keep Carson happy, while Mary thinks secrets are being kept from her(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Foul play disrupts preparations for a charity concert organised to save Maine Woods from dastardly developers intent on lining their own pockets at the expense of the environment. Jessica infiltrates the high-decibel world of rock music to investigate(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris)(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Sister Brigid faces a struggle as she tries to convince her superior at the convent to seek medical help when it becomes obvious she is seriously ill. Jill discovers the answer to a puzzling case, before receiving news from Samantha about the father of her baby. Frankie tells Lizzie his amorous plans, then finds them thwarted when he sees Staff Nurse Taylor with Dr Goodwin. Linda Armstrong, Amy Robbins, Scott Taylor and Michelle Hardwick star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop can barely contain his glee as his beloved Mariette's wedding draws closer, until a summons from the magistrate threatens to turn it into a far from 'perfick' day. Whimsical drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dispirited by a wet English summer, the Larkins cross the Channel in the hope that the French way of life will have a beneficial effect on the family. David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamy's fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. Elsewhere, Vernon decides to venture into the B&B market - but his cooking skills leave a lot to be desired. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps embarks on a mission to make Aidensfield sparkle with his own brand of cleaning products. Drama, starring Aislin McGuckin and Duncan Bell(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis struggle to determine whether a death during a college-based clinical trial for a new antidepressant was murder or suicide, and receive little assistance from the institution's evasive psychiatric professor. A second student's demise then leads to the revelation that the trial's participants are all operating under the influence of a mind-altering drug, and that the lines between love, obsession and madness have become dangerously blurred. Guest starring Douglas Henshall (Primeval), Lucy Liemann (Rev) and Thomas Brodie Sangster (Love Actually)(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: Joanna begins a 2,000-mile journey across Japan in Hokkaido, where she meets one of the most important animals in Japanese culture, the red-crowned crane. She arrives in Sapporo during the middle of the annual Snow Festival and meets members of the local indigenous community, before travelling into the Fukushima exclusion zone and taking a bullet train from Nagano to Tokyo(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: In the wake of the fall of France to the Germans, guests flood into the hotel, including one who catches Lady Hamilton's eye - the Comte de St Claire. Peggy worries about Billy's enthusiasm to sign up as soon as he turns 18, but knows there is little she can do to protect him when his mind looks like it's made up to join his father's regiment, infamously used as canon fodder. When Max faces hostility from the staff, Emma goes out on a limb to defend him - and immediately regrets it(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Joe is offered the opportunity of a lifetime, but finds himself unexpectedly torn as the moral responsibility of staying in England to tell the story of the war weighs heavily on his shoulders. Betsey's mother Gloria comes for a surprise stay, leaving Sonny unsettled and wary of the reaction she incites from Betsey, while Lucian and Lady Hamilton continue their flirtation and Emma anxiously awaits news of Freddie when she hears that a plane from Squadron 392 has gone down(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun
programme.desc: Carter returns to the Valley of the Kings as the First World War draws to a close, hoping to complete his search for the lost tomb. Maggie also arrives in Egypt hoping to resume their relationship, but Carter finds himself smitten with Lord Carnarvon's daughter Evelyn. The excavation is threatened by a tide of Anti-British sentiment sweeping across Egypt, and Carter and Evelyn get caught up in a bombing. Meanwhile, Carnarvon is in financial difficulties, and cannot afford to fund Carter's seemingly hopeless search(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Carol visits Lynn's with a present for Lorraine, Mark refuses to pull his weight on the farm, and Eric wants Michael and Elizabeth to start talking again(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim makes a fuss of Frank before she leaves for her trip with Neil, and Eric sets up Elizabeth for a meeting with Michael, but she refuses to see him(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Neil decide they want to see more of each other, Rachel and Sangeeta's treasure hunt begins, and Archie is not pleased to see Carol back working at the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack has another go at Mark for not working hard on the farm, Lynn encourages Lorraine to do something with her life, and Elizabeth tells Eric about her meeting with Michael(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth suggests to Eric that they start a joint account, Frank sees Kim and Neil together, and Kathy suspects Kim is having an affair(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg is convinced he is going to be fired, Ken is horrified when Deirdre tells him Tracy is not returning to school, and Emily is puzzled by Percy's attitude(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily's story appears in the Gazette, the Duckworths worry that Alf will ask them to repay him all they owe, and the CID warn Mike that they suspect him of fraud(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The bobbies of Aidensfield try to get to the bottom of a series of burglaries in which the culprit only steals certain valuable objects, including Tricia's postcard collection. Meanwhile, Blaketon attempts to raise support for his local election campaign, with surprising results. Rural police drama, starring Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica tackles a case in Osaka, Japan, where a motorcyclist romancing the daughter of a traditionally minded family finds his life on the line. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack are chosen to go on a training course. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack are assigned by Blakey to take a class of rowdy schoolchildren on their annual outing. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan smuggles a woman up to his room, making sure the lights are turned down low so she cannot see how shabby it is - but an unscheduled appearance by her irate father soon puts paid to his dreams of romance. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The family warms to the French way of life, but Charley is unhappy with Mariette's flirting - and after a little Dutch courage, decides to take matters into his own hands. Drama, starring Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paying guests the Langleys arrive for a shooting party at Ashfordly Hall, but their grandiose ideas upset the locals and it is not long before a sinister plot is revealed. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is shocked when she sees Jack's black eye, Eric is not keen on setting up a joint account with Elizabeth, and Kim receives an enormous bouquet of flowers(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lorraine is not pulling her weight at Lynn's, Annie and Sarah fall out over the plan to sell the farm, and Kim tells Neil that Kathy knows everything about their affair(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin is stunned when he is taken in for questioning by the police along with Steve. Deirdre demands more money from Brendan, and Curly lords it over Elaine(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey cannot wait to see the back of the shop, Angie advises Curly to befriend Elaine, and when Tommy gets the mumps, Vera is worried that Jack will catch it(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Tom and Sybbie return to Downton, but the peace is disrupted by an unexpected visit from an old friend. Baxter is faced with a dilemma after an encounter with Sergeant Willis, while Thomas struggles to fill Carson's shoes. Anna goes to London with Mary to deal with a crisis, while her secrets seem likely to be exposed. Daisy is not happy with Cora, and Mary has an admirer(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica enters the exciting world of modern music, risking life, limb and eardrums to investigate the baffling murder of a petty crook. Detective drama, starring Angela Lansbury, Tony Plana and Jason Bernard(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor heads for London to meet the man leading the selection process for the job he wants, but has to conquer his blood phobia once and for all to secure the prestigious position. Back in Cornwall, Aunt Joan locks a mischievous schoolboy in a chicken coop - and faces the consequences when he falls ill. Drama, guest starring Nicholas Le Prevost and Gwen Taylor, with Martin Clunes and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The new midwife in the village persuades Louisa to take a natural, holistic approach to treating an infection, infuriating Martin, who insists on conventional medicine. Meanwhile, Bert is smitten with the flirtatious new cook working in his restaurant, and the doctor hears good news about the job in London. Gemma Craven guest stars, with Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Ian McNeice(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet comes face to face with the man who left her fighting for life just months earlier - serial killer Geoff Hastings, who has vowed to finally reveal the full extent of his crimes. Rachel and the rest of the team investigate the racially motivated murder of a taxi driver. On the personal front, Andy refuses to take no for an answer, so Janet threatens to report him to the DCI. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones, with an appearance by Kevin Doyle (Downton Abbey's Molesley)(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel meets Sean's son, who turns out to be an eight-year-old version of his dad, and the idea of becoming a stepmum puts her further off the idea of marriage than she was before - not that she even agreed in the first place. Andy asks Janet out for a drink, but when she turns him down he accuses her of leading him on. A woman in a sexy cop's uniform turns up dead, and the DCI assumes the killer was her lover - the question is, which one of her many lovers was it? And to add to the mystery, why was her face painted green?(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star(n)

programme.desc: How malt vinegar is brewed, how Aga stoves are made to last a lifetime and a chance to see the work taking place at Britain's last remaining tennis ball factory(n)

Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Roddy McDowall and Ida Lupino(n)

programme.title: River Monsters

For Native Americans it's an ancient and sacred site, but recently something has risen up from the depths to attack swimmers, and stories abound of an invasion of primordial bloodsuckers. programme.title: Treasure Island with Bear Grylls
programme.desc: Twelve people are left on an uninhabited island in the Pacific, marooned and facing the toughest five weeks of their lives, with cash prizes worth £100,000 waiting to be discovered. What the participants do with the money when they find it is up to them, but if they leave early, they leave with nothing. In just the clothes on their back, with a day's supply of water and a few basic tools, the group is abandoned on the treacherous south east side of the island. They are immediately confronted with their first big decision when a helicopter flies overhead and parachutes a box of cash on the north shore. Do they chase the money or try to make camp? Bear keeps a watchful eye on proceedings(n)

programme.title: Adventures in Futureland
programme.desc: Comedian and film-maker Jamali Maddix meets extraordinary characters making a living in the digital era, from Bitcoin millionaires to children supporting their families by uploading viral content. In the first edition, Jamali travels to the US, where he meets a new breed of porn star known as cammers, who perform directly for their clients via their laptops. He visits a studio in Las Vegas, where 30 women between the ages of 18 and 60 work round the clock, offering a range of services from simple friendship to incest fantasies(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Following the convictions of serial killer Stephen Port and murderer Gerald Matovu, who both used GHB as their weapon, Dispatches has conducted an eight-month investigation with BuzzFeed News into the use of the drug among gay men. With the largest ever survey of GHB users, Dispatches reveals for the first time the human cost of the epidemic scale of abuse, with over a quarter of users reporting being sexually assaulted, almost half having overdosed, thousands of hospital admissions, and a hidden crime spree: rape, murder, and the live-streaming of sexual abuse. The law allows GBL to be sold for cleaning purposes, which enables easy access to industrial quantities of it online, which can then be used/sold as GHB to users. Dispatches interviews men who have been deliberately overdosed, raped, and those including Lord Brian Paddick who have lost partners and family members(n)

programme.desc: The actor visits York Minster, which over the past 800 years, has been a backdrop to some of the most dramatic events in British history. It houses more than half of all the world's medieval glass, including the Rose Window, commissioned by Henry VIII, and the East Window, the largest stained glass window in the world. A succession of outspoken bishops of York have defied Nazis, married kings and queens, and more recently championed equality with Archbishop John Sentamu, the country's first black bishop, consecrating the world's first female bishop, Libby Lane, in 2014.(n)

Plus, the stories of a Border collie mix who finds survivors during earthquakes, a labrador-German Shepherd mix who helps stop ivory poaching, and a team of water rescue dogs in Italy(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet

Julian is called out in the middle of the night to save a cow that has experienced dangerous complications while giving birth, a pig is resistant to being castrated, and a dog swallows a bottle of e-cigarette liquid(n)

programme.title: Suicidal
programme.desc: Documentary following six men who have considered killing themselves or even attempted it as they undergo treatment at Riverside Mental Health Centre in Hillingdon, London. The programme explores the mental health issues that have driven them to this point, including bereavement, isolation, a struggle to adapt to a changing world, or long-standing trauma caused by childhood bullying(n)

programme.title: How You Can Help Stop Suicide
programme.desc: Advice on how to help people who may be considering killing themselves, exploring the warning signs to look for and how to engage with the subject safely. Featuring contributions from medical professionals and people who have recovered from suicide attempts, and gone on to help others(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life Of...
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a major UK hospital revealing the vast network of processes and people that work alongside the frontline medical staff. They include microbiologists, fighting infection on thousands of bed sheets, surgeons' scrubs, life-saving medical equipment and hospital surfaces. The film also meets the staff who care for the patients who do not get to go home - those who work in the morgue - and visits the hip bone repository, where one person's job is to grind human knee parts to save lives in spinal surgery(n)

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go! (1966)

programme.title: Shark Tale (2004)
programme.desc: Ambitious fish Oscar dreams of escaping his humdrum undersea life. But Oscar soon finds himself out of his depth as his fearsome reputation gets out of control. Animated comedy, with the voices of Will Smith, Robert De Niro, Martin Scorsese, Renee Zellweger, Angelina Jolie and Jack Black(n)

programme.title: Carry On Nurse (1959)

Feeling aggrieved by her no-nonsense methods, the chaps decide the time is right to get their own back. Comedy, with Kenneth Williams, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey, Shirley Eaton and Leslie Phillips(n)

programme.title: One Fine Day (1996)

The pair agree to take it in turns to babysit while struggling to stick to their hectic schedules - but during the course of the day their feelings for each other blossom unexpectedly. Romantic comedy, starring George Clooney, Michelle Pfeiffer, Charles Durning and Mae Whitman(n)

programme.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 (2014)

Part one of the two-part conclusion to the sci-fi adventure series, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Philip Seymour Hoffman, Julianne Moore and Donald Sutherland(n)

Comedy, starring Melissa McCarthy, Kristen Bell and Peter Dinklage(n)

Romantic comedy drama, starring an Oscar-winning Jennifer Lawrence, with Bradley Cooper and Robert De Niro(n)

Seafaring adventure, with Fred MacMurray, Vera Ralston and Robert Douglas(n)

Western, starring Randolph Scott, Alexander Knox, Joan Leslie and Ellen Drew(n)

Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims(n)

programme.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2 (2015)

Conclusion of the sci-fi adventure series based on Suzanne Collins' novels, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Donald Sutherland and Philip Seymour Hoffman in his final role(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Spider monkey Mary is 50 and has had 19 children, and the keepers worry that another pregnancy could be risky, so they introduce younger female Kiara to the group in the hope that Mary's long-term partner Popoyan will develop a relationship with her instead. The staff also hope to breed dominant female giant otter Icana with new arrival Diego and plan to bring two feuding sloths closer together, while setting up a speed-dating event for three-legged chameleon Tripod(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick takes a look at 12-year-old cat Pixie, who has been hit by a car and brought in by owner Diane. A Great Dane called Ellie has deformed bones and discs pressing down on her spinal cord, but Noel hopes to relieve the dog's pain by placing bone marrow taken from her shoulder into a special scaffolding in her neck, helping the vertebrae fuse together. programme.title: The Supervet

programme.title: Prince Philip: The Plot to Make a King
programme.desc: Secret History documentary telling the inside story of the dynastic tensions that were unleashed when Princess Elizabeth, the future queen, fell in love with Prince Philip. It was felt that Philip was rough and ill-mannered - and with the Second World War still fresh in most people's minds, the royal and political elite disliked his German roots. But following the death of King George VI in 1952, the establishment feared a dynastic coup, with the Queen torn between the two sides. The documentary examines the complexities and frustrations of the royal marriage, as the monarch struggled to reconcile the love of her husband with the suspicions of her family and her government(n)

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe

programme.title: Time Team Specials

Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby(n)

programme.title: Skeletons of the Mary Rose: The New Evidence
programme.desc: Scientists and historians use isotope and DNA analysis to identify the crew of Henry VIII's flagship, which sank off the coast of Portsmouth in 1545. With the help of Mary Rose Trust curator Alex Hildred, the programme uncovers evidence that many of the sailors were of African origin, and traces surviving descendants of the only two members of the crew who can be identified by name. Plus, the little-known story of a 16th-century Guinean free-diver who retrieved several treasures from the wreck(n)

programme.title: Treasure Island with Bear Grylls
programme.desc: Twelve people are left on an uninhabited island in the Pacific, marooned and facing the toughest five weeks of their lives, with cash prizes worth £100,000 waiting to be discovered. What the participants do with the money when they find it is up to them, but if they leave early, they leave with nothing. In just the clothes on their back, with a day's supply of water and a few basic tools, the group is abandoned on the treacherous south east side of the island. They are immediately confronted with their first big decision when a helicopter flies overhead and parachutes a box of cash on the north shore. Do they chase the money or try to make camp?(n)

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Comic writer and actor Stephen Mangan pays a visit to the cafe at the end of Southend Pier, where Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty recreate one of the best dishes he's ever had, which was while he followed the English cricket team around the world in the Nasser Hussain era. They also deliver a signed cricket bat that Stephen puts to unusual use in the kitchen. Plus, Jamie makes fresh pasta ravioli with delicious radicchio, balsamic, potato and fontina, Jimmy creates the perfect build to cook roast dinner, even when on the move and the duo explore wild British venison(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, the first host, radio DJ and gospel singer Sandra, plans a night full of good vibes and joy. However, her cooking doesn't impress foodie Craig, recruitment manager Fraser or theatrical, musical-loving Debbie. On night two Debbie delivers drama, but not quite in the way she planned. Marina owner Craig hosts night three on a houseboat. Finally, suave youngster Fraser pulls an impromptu serenade from nowhere, but will it be enough to win him the £1,000 cash prize?(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: A composer and expectant father intends to build a home with a recording studio and performance stage, but is already over budget(n)

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver goes on a mission to change the way people think about vegetables, demonstrating a collection of vibrant, hearty and healthy vegetarian dishes that everyone can add to their kitchen. In the first edition, he kicks things off with a cottage pie with a rich gravy and super-fluffy mash, is inspired by Indian street food, then makes a chip butty packed full of flavour and crunch. He goes back to school to meet children who are celebrating vegetables, and makes macaroni and cheese full of greens(n)

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. programme.title: The Stuarts in Exile
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which Dr Clare Jackson recalls Prince James Francis Stuart's audacious and long-running rebellion to reclaim his throne from the Hanoverian king George I. She tells the story of the Stuarts in exile and sheds new light on the political, military and cultural threat the Jacobites posed to the embryonic British state, polarising public opinion in the process(n)

programme.title: Battle of Britain: The Real Story
programme.desc: James Holland explores the Battle of Britain from the German point of view. The author focuses on the tactics, technology and intelligence available to both nations, and examines how they decided to use their resources. He also gains access to first-hand testimonies from German pilots, and diaries and documents that reveal a previously unknown side to this confrontation(n)

programme.title: Strictly Confidential (1959)
programme.desc: Two conmen fresh out of prison are given prestigious jobs by a widow - not realising she has a devious scheme in mind. Comedy, starring Richard Murdoch, William Kendall, William Hartnell and Maya Koumani(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the cliffs at Flamborough Head and through Clumber Park, Nottinghamshire(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Joey Bones selects his favourite moments from the series that cuts straight to the core of London's food scene(n)

programme.title: Stephen Fry's Key to the City
programme.desc: The QI host tours the City of London, discovering the hidden mysteries of this rich and powerful square mile. Along the way, he visits the Bank of England's vaults, witnesses high drama at the London Metal Exchange as dealers buy and sell stocks, and experiences Dead Man's Walk at the Old Bailey, where many condemned criminals trod their final steps. Plus, as a recipient of the Freedom of the City of London, Stephen finds out just what privileges this gives him(n)

programme.title: The Only Way (1970)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama, set in occupied Denmark and telling the story of a heroic effort to smuggle 7,000 Jews over the border into Sweden so they might escape the Holocaust. Starring Ove Sprogoe and Jane Seymour(n)

programme.title: The Gentle Gunman (1952)
programme.desc: A member of the IRA is sent to London to help with a campaign of bombing, but becomes disillusioned with the organisation's violent methods. His desire to further the republican cause through peaceful means fuels tension with his brother and leads to him being branded a traitor. Drama, starring John Mills and Dirk Bogarde(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Food busker John Quilter makes smoked salmon tagliatelle, while DJ BBQ gets stuck into some American-style ribs. Plus, the Soul Food Sisters host a supper club in Tottenham(n)

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events showcasing London's vibrant culture scene(n)

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Luftwaffe chief and designated successor to Hitler, Hermann Goering. The larger-than-life character was renowned for his charisma and flamboyance, but also as one of the most unrepentant and ruthless of all Nazis. Having aimed to use the courtroom as a means of rehabilitating the fascist regime, it was only at a late stage that he was forced to concede defeat. Robert Pugh stars(n)

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: The English Teacher (2013)
programme.desc: A teacher is reunited with her favourite past pupil, who has returned from New York having failed to succeed as a playwright and under pressure from his family to get a job. She puts her reputation on the line to prove her protege has talent by getting the school to put on a production of his play. Comedy drama, starring Julianne Moore, Michael Angarano and Greg Kinnear(n)

programme.title: What's Cooking? (2000)
programme.desc: The members of four diverse families meet up in their respective homes to celebrate Thanksgiving, where the growing Americanisation of the younger generation becomes ever more apparent to their parents, and tangled emotional battles are played out. Drama exploring multiculturalism in Los Angeles, starring Dennis Haysbert, Joan Chen, Julianna Margulies and Kyra Sedgewick(n)

programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Luftwaffe chief and designated successor to Hitler, Hermann Goering. The larger-than-life character was renowned for his charisma and flamboyance, but also as one of the most unrepentant and ruthless of all Nazis. Having aimed to use the courtroom as a means of rehabilitating the fascist regime, it was only at a late stage that he was forced to concede defeat. Robert Pugh stars(n)

programme.title: Malta Story (1953)
programme.desc: The island of Malta comes under siege during the Second World War, as both Allied and Axis forces see it as vital to their supply lines. A British reconnaissance pilot arrives on the island, and plays a crucial role in defending it from the advancing enemy forces. Drama, starring Alec Guinness, Anthony Steel, Jack Hawkins, Muriel Pavlow and Flora Robson(n)

programme.title: Blind Corner (1964)
programme.desc: A composer's wife learns her husband plans to cut her out of his will, so conspires with her lover to murder him first. Crime drama, starring William Sylvester and Barbara Shelley(n)

programme.desc: Major Ken Hames examines events surrounding the 1980 Iranian Embassy siege in London, during which six Arab terrorists held more than 20 hostages at gunpoint until SAS forces stormed the building, freeing the captives and killing five of their captors(n)

programme.title: The Wind of Change (1961)
programme.desc: An unemployed teenager in Notting Hill grows increasingly frustrated with his life, his anger manifesting as a deep-seated racism. When he and his friends commit a brutal act of violence on a black man and his white girlfriend, the attack has devastating repercussions for his family. Drama, starring Donald Pleasence and Johnny Briggs(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: On the eve of the withdrawal from Afghanistan of most American, British and other Nato forces, Panorama examines a Taliban stronghold just 60 miles from the capital Kabul. Nagieb Khaja, who has reported from Afghanistan for 10 years, reveals a hidden and dangerous world, where strict sharia law is enforced. It is from here Taliban forces are now targeting the capital itself(n)

programme.title: Warrior Challenge
programme.desc: MMA & K1 from the Troxy in London title fight Mihai Zmarandescu vs Alfie Hawes plus Erol Raif vs George Zorilov, Kat Freeman vs Kat Twardowska(n)

programme.desc: Danny Dyer investigates clubs in the north west of England, including Manchester United and Liverpool, and looks at how racism plays a part in violence(n)

programme.title: Bonded By Blood 2: The Next Generation (2017)
programme.desc: A gangland shooting leaves the London underworld with a dangerous power vacuum. A thriving drug kingpin, a petty thief striving to be accepted and an unstable criminal are all determined to seize that power for themselves, tearing their friendship apart in the process. Crime drama, starring Sam Strike, Josh Myers and George Russo(n)

programme.title: Education Anglaise (1983)
programme.desc: An orphan is enrolled in a secretive school by her lecherous uncle, and discovers that the teachers run the establishment with strict discipline, while the students are all interested in expanding their sexual horizons. programme.desc: Cherry Healey presents the second of two programmes in which she explores the unexpected positive health benefits of everyday foods. programme.title: Strictly Confidential (1959)

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: How to Stay Young
programme.desc: In the second of two documentaries about beating the ageing process, Angela Rippon and Dr Chris van Tulleken find out what people can do to give their brains a boost. Angela travels to Japan, where she discovers the foods that can help protect your brain, and meets the 100-year-old who is living proof of the importance of diet in keeping the grey matter healthy. In the USA, Chris finds out about a trial in which scientists are hoping to beat Alzheimer's disease by injecting sufferers with the blood of young people(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)
